message:
fix(core,extent,types,alloc): eliminate silent truncation, panics, and division-by-zero

Four bug categories fixed:

1. Silent u64→u32 truncation in indirect block pointers: All BlockNumber.0
   casts now use try_from with explicit error instead of silent `as u32`.
   On 64-bit ext4 filesystems (>16 TiB), allocated blocks beyond u32::MAX
   would have produced corrupt indirect pointers.

2. Division-by-zero in block_to_group/inode_to_group: Added zero guards
   for blocks_per_group and inodes_per_group. Corrupted superblocks with
   zero values would panic instead of returning a graceful error.

3. expect() panics in map_logical_to_physical: Replaced 4 expect() calls
   with map_err returning InvalidGeometry. Large count values that push
   the logical position past u32::MAX now return errors instead of panicking.

4. Potential underflow in succinct bitmap select0: Changed 3 arithmetic
   operations to use saturating_sub for defense-in-depth against index
   corruption.

Also fixed 5 `block_dev` immutability errors from concurrent agent's
block-device re-acquisition commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
